ddls:
CREATE TABLE "clusters" (
  "cluster_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each cluster */,
  "cluster_name" TEXT /* Name of the cluster */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the cluster */,
  "cluster_type" TEXT /* Type of the cluster (e.g., primary, secondary) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the cluster (e.g., on-premises, cloud) */,
  " infrastructure_type" TEXT /* Type of infrastructure (e.g., virtual, physical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "objects" (
  "object_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each object */,
  "object_name" TEXT /* Name of the object */,
  "object_type" TEXT /* Type of the object (e.g., VM, Cluster) */,
  "object_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the object */,
  "object_category" TEXT /* Category of the object (e.g., network device, server) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("object_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "errors" (
  "error_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each error */,
  "error_code" TEXT /* Code representing the error */,
  "error_reason" TEXT /* Descriptive reason for the error */,
  "error_type" TEXT /* Type of the error (e.g., system, application) */,
  "error_category" TEXT /* Category of the error (e.g., network, database) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the incident */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the incident was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location of access (e.g., internal, external) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "threat_classes" (
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each class of threat */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the threat class */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the threat class */,
  "class_type" TEXT /* Type of the threat class (e.g., malware, phishing) */,
  "class_category" TEXT /* Category of the threat class (e.g., network, application) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the source (e.g., system, network) */,
  "source_category" TEXT /* Category of the source (e.g., internal, external) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type of incident */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the incident type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the incident type */,
  "type_category" TEXT /* Category of the incident type (e.g., security, compliance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_severity" (
  "severity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each severity level */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Level of severity (e.g., warning, critical) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the severity level */,
  "severity_category" TEXT /* Category of the severity level (e.g., high, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("severity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_response" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident response */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident being responded to */,
  "response_time" TEXT /* Time when the incident response was initiated */,
  "response_actions" TEXT /* Actions taken during the incident response */,
  "response_outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the incident response (e.g., successful, unsuccessful) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident report */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident being reported on */,
  "report_time" TEXT /* Time when the incident report was generated */,
  "report_details" TEXT /* Details of the incident report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "knowledge_base" (
  "kb_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each knowledge base entry */,
  "kb_title" TEXT /* Title of the knowledge base entry */,
  "kb_description" TEXT /* Description of the knowledge base entry */,
  "kb_content" TEXT /* Content of the knowledge base entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("kb_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident history entry */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident being tracked */,
  "history_time" TEXT /* Time when the incident history entry was made */,
  "history_details" TEXT /* Details of the incident history entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id")
)
